title: Sanger Tree of Life HMW DNA Extraction: Automated Plant MagAttract v.3
author: Graeme Oatley

steps_raw.instruction: Prepare a lysis buffer master mix: %s %s ReagentVolume per samplePhosphate buffered saline (PBS)200 ¬ĶLProteinase K20 ¬ĶLRNase A4 ¬ĶLBuffer AL150 ¬ĶL
Set a heat block to 55 ¬įC.
Transfer 50 mg of cryogenically homogenised tissue from each sample to 2 mL microcentrifuge tubes and place on dry ice to keep the samples frozen.

Whilst samples are lysing, label nine 1 mL 96-well deep-well KingFisher‚ĄĘ plates and fill the number of wells required for the number of samples in each plate as follows: %s %s PlateReagent(s) requiredTip plate96-well tip comb (no reagent)Elution 2 200 ¬ĶL Buffer AE Elution 1 200 ¬ĶL Buffer AE NFW Wash 500 ¬ĶL Nuclease-Free Water PE Wash 2700 ¬ĶL Buffer PEPE Wash 1700 ¬ĶL Buffer PEMW1 Wash 2700 ¬ĶL Buffer MW1MW1 Wash 1700 ¬ĶL Buffer MW1Sample plate15 ¬ĶL Suspension G magnetic beads 280 ¬ĶL Buffer MB
Centrifuge tube briefly to collect, then incubate on the heat block at 55 ¬įC at 600 rpm for 1 hour.
Once samples have completed lysing, remove sample tubes from the heat block and briefly centrifuge to spin down.

Using a wide-bore pipette tip, set the volume to 380 ¬ĶL, transfer lysate from the sample tubes to individual wells in the sample plate, taking care not to transfer large pieces of debris if possible.

Select the required DNA extraction protocol in the protocol list on the KingFisher‚ĄĘ Apex (details in KingFisher‚ĄĘ Apex DNA Extraction Protocol Script/attached KFX file in the Materials section) and select using the play button.
Load the filled plates onto the instrument following the instructions provided on screen.

Prior to loading the ‚ÄúSample Plate‚ÄĚ, the instrument will prompt to remove the ‚ÄúTip Plate‚ÄĚ. Once the final plate is loaded, the protocol will automatically begin; this takes approximately 50 minutes.
Once the protocol has completed, follow the on-screen instructions to remove plates from the instrument.

Inspect the elution plates for any magnetic beads in the wells. In the rare instance of magnetic beads remaining in the eluate (possible in viscous samples), these samples will need to be transferred to a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube and placed on a magnetic rack. Allow around 5 minutes for the beads to migrate and take the clear eluate containing the DNA using a wide-bore pipette tip.

Using a 200 ¬ĶL multi-channel pipette and wide-bore tips, pipette eluates from Elution Plate 2 into Elution Plate 1, and gently pipette mix 5‚Äď10 times with wide-bore tips to fully homogenise DNA in the eluate. Elution Plate 1 with the combined eluates is now the ‚ÄėSample Plate‚Äô for the 0.45X SPRI.

Set-up the KingFisher plates for the 0.45X SPRI as detailed below: %s %s PlatePlate TypeReagent(s) requiredTip Plate1 mL deep-well96-well tip comb (no reagent)Sample Plate (Elution Plate 1 from DNA Extraction Protocol)1 mL deep-well380 ¬ĶL DNA + 171 ¬ĶL AMPure PB beadsEthanol Wash Plate1 mL deep-well1000 ¬ĶL 80% EtOH (freshly made)Elution Plate200 ¬ĶL standard135 ¬ĶL Buffer EB

Select the required 0.45X SPRI protocol in the protocol list on the KingFisher‚ĄĘ Apex (details in KingFisher‚ĄĘ Apex 0.45X SPRI Protocol Script/attached KFX file in the Material section) and select using the play button.
Load the filled plates onto the instrument following the instructions provided on screen.
Once the final plate is loaded, the protocol will automatically begin; this will take approximately 40 minutes.
Once the protocol has completed, follow the on-screen instructions to remove plates from the instrument.
Using a wide-bore pipette tip, transfer the 130 ¬ĶL of eluate from the elution plate into microcentrifuge tubes.
Incubate the DNA at room temperature overnight and perform the required QC the following morning.
Add 374 ¬ĶL of the lysis buffer master mix to each sample, then homogenise sample and mastermix by gently pipetting 10 times with a wide-bore pipette tip.
Store the DNA at 4 ¬įC.
